description.value:
The top of the line cyberchair, giving the premier user many options. While using it the operator's base MOVE becomes 5, with all regular movement options remaining available.

Additionally, this cyberchair has room for 4 cyberlimb options*. Any options installed always count as being paired.

Operation of the cyberchair requires either a free hand, or interface plugs.

If the operator suffers a critical injury that reduces their MOVE, the MOVE of the cyberchair is instead effected.

The cyberchair cannot be targeted specifically, nor can it be disabled via EMP effects.

*Installing cyberlimp options currently not supported
Access to nervous system.
AI Smartphone. +2 Library Search, +2 Wardrobe & Style depending on circumstance.

Intended as a direct substitute/improvement to regular armor, to compensate for ablation changes.

-1 to REF, DEX, and MOVE - these penalties do not stack with those for the helmet, and are removed by a BODY stat of 7 or higher (change the armor penalty stat in the item settings tab)..

Intended as a direct substitute/improvement to regular armor, to compensate for ablation changes.

-1 to REF, DEX, and MOVE - these penalties do not stack with those for the helmet, and are removed by a BODY stat of 7 or higher (change the armor penalty stat in the item settings tab)..
A standard-issue assault rifle.
Increases magazine size to a hefty 35 bullets.
A sight midification for any ranged weapon. Provides +1 to hit against targets within 12m.
Basic ammo, nothing special.
Business executives and fat-cat defenders ready to risk it all to rise the corporate ladder and realize the New American dream.
+2 Personal Grooming when using Techhair as well. Changes hue.
+2 Grooming with Chemskin. Color changing hair.
Glowing tattoos. +2 to Wardrobe & Style if you have at least three installed.
Provides an LED showing vitals readouts. Can be linked to your agent.
The most technological and flashy clothing you can find in the Time of the Red.
The most technological and flashy clothing you can find in the Time of the Red.
Your typical corporative clothing. Show everyone that you're here and you mean business.
Your typical corporative clothing. Show everyone that you're here and you mean business.
Your typical corporative clothing. Show everyone that you're here and you mean business.
Your typical corporative clothing. Show everyone that you're here and you mean business.

information.notes:
The Tank's storied history begins in the NSSR with his parents' divorce. Mom wanted to leave the capitol and head to Siberia to settle in the new nomad farming communities there. Dad wanted to stay and endorse the corporate hard line, and for Nikolai to take the SovOil-sponsored uni scholarhsip. Turns out, Dad could afford better lawyers.

After a successful 4 years as the star of the university's rugby team, Nikolai graduated and began his career at SovOil by promptly stabbing his sponsor in the back and ascending into the ranks of middle and upper management like he was fired from a cannon during Tchaikovsky's Overture. A long string of similar espionage - both within the company and against its enemies - culminated in his transfer to Night City during the late 2030s… where he lost his legs and his status when he went up against Silvers the Solo and found out the hard way what a real professional looks like. The company offered him a choice: a pair of state-of-the-art cyberlegs, or the latest from Rocklin Augmentics. For a man named the Tank, it was hardly a choice.

Unfortunately, lately Nikolai's been ground under the heel of one of the very people he tried to ruin on his way up the ladder, and they haven't forgotten him. This was readily apparent when, after his latest op went wrong, they took his corporate-issued interface plugs, cannon, armor, cyberchair upgrades, and threatened further should he fail again. They're likely betting on it.

Difficulty: Medium, due to having no single clear strength, but gets easier the more gear you'll be able to accumulate. Understanding how powerful your assistant's loaned action economy is will be essential.

Style of Play: Nikolai is good at just about everything that doesn't require him to think, thanks to being able to dump MOVE with impunity. How you build him is up to you, but he has an excellent framework for a frontline fighter dealing with heavy weapons, and grooming his chosen companion(s) into a formidable force.

Ask your GM if Nikolai is allowed to dodge bullets, given his reliance on a cyberchair.

Riding points are allocated on the assumption that they're used to maneuver the cyberchair; ask your GM if they plan to utilize such checks, and if not, reallocate. The same can be said for his 2 extra ranks in evasion.

Character Goals: Get out from under your awful manager at SovOil before they become the death of you. Acquire all of the latest gadgetry and reclaim/repurchase your missing equipment, from your heavy weapon(s) of choice to your chair upgrades, interface plugs, heavy armor, etc.

Cyberware to Aspire to: Cyberleg enhancements work very well for the spider chair. Nikolai himself would take well to a muscle/bone graft and linear frame for extra HP - check out the options in Black Chrome, and ask your GM if their bonuses can be granted to the chair, while still retaining the BODY boost that they provide. Make room for borgware by relying on external gadgets (smart glasses, battle gloves) instead of implants.

In-Character Voice & Acting Inspiration: Rather original, this one.

Local Expert: Executive Zone

Chem Skin: Gives his skin a metallic sheen.

Techhair: Glossy and black, typically slick and greased.

Light Tats: Has designs all across his body that give the impression of more integrated cyberware than he actually has.

All edgerunners included within NCG&C are wholly original, made to be played out of the box, and created using the "Complete Package" rules found in RED's Core Rulebook.

lifepath.culturalOrigin: NeoSoviet Russia - a true rags-to-riches story... +/- the corruption allegations
lifepath.personality: Quick to anger, quick to laugh, and like many other NeoSovs, materialistic to a fault - absolutely obsessed with the latest and greatest tech.
lifepath.clothingStyle: A sharp, tech-augmented business suit and extensive body cosmetics.
lifepath.affectations: His cyberchair. He has functional legs... technically, but why when chair?
lifepath.valueMost: The absolute latest in shiny gadgetry, from fashion to function.
lifepath.aboutPeople: Worthwhile in the context of collection - as connections, tools, conquests, or warnings.
lifepath.valuedPerson: Himself, always, though he usually picks someone special out of the pack to groom.
lifepath.valuedPossession: Again, cyberchair - it literally gives him legs to stand on.
lifepath.familyBackground: Grew up in the burgeoning middle class in the west of the NSSR (New Soviet Socialist Republic). Parents were repair techs.
lifepath.childhoodEnvironment: Bolstered by the NSSR's recovery, Nikolai received a full primary education in the most threadbare and utilitarian conditions possible.
lifepath.familyCrisis: Parents divorced - father demanded a corporate hard-line for Nikolai's future, mother wanted ot move to Siberia to join the nomad settlers forming farming communities.
lifepath.lifeGoals: Get out from under his oppressive manager before the jobs he's being forced into get him killed.
lifepath.friends: An influential rockerboy that throws the hottest parties in Pacifica.
lifepath.tragicLoveAffairs: A long string of beautiful women, most of them just as shallow and materialistic as Nikolai.
lifepath.enemies: An exec (likely Petrochem or SovOil, could be anyone) who's become an obstacle to Nikolai's status in Night City
